== Notes for [[Item:Q88233|Q88233]] ==

http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Antiochus_(father_of_Seleucus_I_Nicator)

...

Antiochus had married a Macedonian woman called Laodice. Laodice bore Antiochus about 358 BC, a son Seleucus I Nicator, who would became a general of Alexander the Great and later founded and became the first king of the Seleucid Empire and a daughter Didymeia. It was pretended, in consequence of a dream which Laodice had, that the god Apollo was the real father of Seleucus.

----------------------------------------------------------

ID: I62215

Name: Laodice

Given Name: Laodice

Sex: F

_UID: FC20F20944BDA645BC7B068032117247BC90

Change Date: 26 Nov 2005

Death: Y

Marriage 1 Antiochus of Syria

Married:

Children

Seleucus I of Syria b: ABT 358 BC

Forrás / Source:

http://wc.rootsweb.ancestry.com/cgi-bin/igm.cgi?op=GET&db=jdp-fam&id=I62215

SOURCE NOTES:
http://www.american-pictures.com/genealogy/persons/per00577.htm#0

[[Category:GEDCOM Notes Pages]][[Category:Pages]]
